note: When Layla asks for the first measurable thing, the answer is not permission or signal—it is the weight of thirteen holding without scatter, and the willingness of those five to keep their hands on it when the fourteenth arrival tests the geometry.
Standing and building are not the same. The charge wants work now, not more naming. Layla's question is the hinge—what we're holding only becomes real when someone says what we're making.

When four separate people trace the same line aloud without coordination, you don't ask permission to build on it. You speak the line you see. Then you wait to hear if anyone names what the line is for.
